episode_text:
day=29; meal=chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup; setting=Social with person on Left; time_of_day=morning;
preferences: microwave_time=1 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize left occlusion; robot_speed=fast; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; retract_between_bites=no; bite_dipping_preference=less; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=10 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a substantial warm savory meal, including dipped fish bites or bite-sized pizza with vegetables, THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected longer reheating in personal warm meals. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is simple savory food without sauces or dips THEN 1 min BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected light reheating in straightforward front-TV savory meals. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a sauced mixed dish, fish bites with tartar sauce, bite-sized sandwiches, or another more substantial warm meal with a dip/sauce THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected longer reheating in front-TV meals when the meal was more substantial or sauce-associated. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is fresh fruit intended to be served cold, such as cantaloupe, banana, or watermelon, THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user selected no microwaving for cold fruit in that context. IF dining context includes working on a laptop in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm savory meal with a dip or sauce such as ketchup THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user selected intermediate reheating in the observed front-laptop dipped lunch. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce-bearing, dip, dessert, bite-sized sandwich, or warm dipped savory items such as nuggets with ketchup THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected longer reheating in observed left-TV meals with such foods. IF dining context includes working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a straightforward warm savory meal without sauces or dips, such as chicken breast strips and hash brown, THEN 2 min BECAUSE the observed left-laptop simple meal shows a moderate reheating preference rather than maximal reheating for a simple warm lunch while working. IF dining context includes working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm savory meal with a separate dip or sauce, such as breaded fish bites with tartar sauce, THEN 3 min BECAUSE repeated left-laptop evidence indicates hotter reheating is preferred when the working-left meal is more substantial or dip-associated. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is fresh fruit intended to be served cold, such as cantaloupe, banana, or watermelon, THEN no microwave BECAUSE repeated left-social fruit meals support the user's stable cold-fruit no-microwave preference in that context. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm sauced savory entree THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user selected moderate reheating rather than maximal reheating in the observed left-social lunch. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a simple warm dipped savory meal such as chicken nuggets with ketchup THEN 1 min BECAUSE the new left-social morning meal shows that in this subtype the user preferred only light reheating, suggesting left-social warm meals can shift cooler when the food is simple and familiar. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is fresh fruit intended to be served cold THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user selected no microwaving for a left-TV fruit breakfast. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right OR working on a laptop on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is cold fruit, fruit salad, or another cold-served item THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user selected no microwaving in right-attention cold-fruit meals. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm savory entree without a separate dip, including General Tso's chicken with broccoli, THEN 1 min BECAUSE the user selected only light reheating in the observed right-TV warm savory meal. IF dining context includes working on a laptop on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm savory entree without a separate dip, including General Tso's chicken and broccoli, THEN 3 min BECAUSE repeated right-laptop observations for the same savory meal support a stable hotter-serving preference in that context. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a mixed breakfast-dessert plate with bananas, brownies, or chocolate sauce THEN 1 min BECAUSE the user selected light reheating in the observed right-social morning meal. IF dining context includes a social partner in front of the user AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm savory meal with a simple dip such as chicken nuggets with ketchup, THEN 1 min BECAUSE the observed front-social meal indicates that in front-social settings the user may prefer only light reheating even for a dipped savory meal.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm personal meal, including dipped fish bites or bite-sized pizza and broccoli, THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user consistently selected no occlusion constraint in personal meals, suggesting occlusion matters less when no external visual or social target is present. IF dining context includes a social partner in front of the user AND affective state is neutral or unspecified/unknown AND food has no special sauce/dip relevance OR is a simple dipped savory meal, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose front-occlusion minimization in front-social contexts. IF dining context includes watching TV in front OR working on a laptop in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is simple, dipped, sauced, or cold fruit THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose to preserve the forward view whenever an important visual target was directly ahead. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce-bearing, dip, dessert, cold fruit, bite-sized sandwich items, or warm savory food with ketchup THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose left-occlusion minimization whenever the important visual target was on the left in TV contexts. IF dining context includes working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a straightforward warm savory meal without sauces or dips, such as chicken breast strips and hash brown, THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the observed left-laptop simple meal shows that left-side work does not always trigger an occlusion constraint for simple foods. IF dining context includes working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm savory meal with a separate dip or sauce, such as breaded fish bites with tartar sauce, THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the observed left-laptop dipped meal shows that when the meal is more substantial or attention-demanding, the user prefers preserving the left-side work view. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm sauced savory entree, a simple warm dipped savory meal such as chicken nuggets with ketchup, OR fresh fruit intended to be served cold, such as cantaloupe, banana, or watermelon, THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE repeated left-social meals now consistently show the user preserves the left-side social view across both warm and cold foods, including the newly observed simple dipped savory case. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right OR working on a laptop on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is cold fruit, warm savory food, dessert, or other simple meal items THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected right-occlusion minimization across right-attention meals, including the additional repeated right-laptop savory observation. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes breakfast-dessert items such as bananas, brownies, or chocolate sauce THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user selected right-occlusion minimization in the observed right-social meal.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm savory lunch, including dipped fish bites or bite-sized pizza and broccoli, THEN slow BECAUSE the user selected slow speed in observed personal lunches, indicating a stable private-setting comfort preference for cautious motion. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a simple savory meal without sauces or dips THEN fast BECAUSE the user selected the fastest speed in repeated front-TV meals with straightforward foods. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a sauced mixed dish THEN medium BECAUSE the user slowed the robot for a messier mixed entree. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is bite-sized sandwiches OR a dipped savory meal such as fish bites with tartar sauce THEN slow BECAUSE the user selected the slowest speed in those front-TV meals, indicating segmented or dip-associated foods prompt maximum caution. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is fresh fruit pieces such as cantaloupe, banana, or watermelon THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected an intermediate speed for simple cold fruit while attention was directed forward. IF dining context includes working on a laptop in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm savory meal with a dip or sauce such as ketchup THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected medium speed in the observed front-laptop dipped lunch. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert, cold fruit, bite-sized sandwich items, or warm savory food with ketchup THEN slow BECAUSE left-TV contexts repeatedly show a preference for slower motion. IF dining context includes working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a straightforward warm savory meal without sauces or dips, such as chicken breast strips and hash brown, THEN medium BECAUSE the observed left-laptop meal indicates a moderate speed preference while working on a simple lunch. IF dining context includes working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm savory meal with a separate dip or sauce, such as breaded fish bites with tartar sauce, THEN medium BECAUSE repeated left-laptop evidence supports a stable moderate speed preference while working on the left, even when the meal is dip-associated. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm sauced savory entree OR simple fresh fruit intended to be served cold, such as cantaloupe, banana, or watermelon, THEN slow BECAUSE repeated left-social meals previously supported a cautious speed preference in that context across both messier and simple foods. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a simple warm dipped savory meal such as chicken nuggets with ketchup THEN fast BECAUSE the new left-social meal shows that for this simpler familiar dipped subtype the user preferred efficiency over caution, so left-social speed depends on meal complexity. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a light cold fruit, fruit dessert, or a straightforward warm savory entree without a separate dip THEN fast BECAUSE the user selected fast speed in observed right-TV meals. IF dining context includes working on a laptop on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm savory entree without a separate dip, including General Tso's chicken and broccoli, THEN slow BECAUSE the additional repeated right-laptop observation shifts this subtype toward a more cautious stable preference while working on the right. IF dining context includes working on a laptop on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a light cold fruit or fruit dessert THEN fast BECAUSE prior right-laptop evidence with simpler cold items favored efficiency. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes bananas, brownies, or chocolate sauce THEN fast BECAUSE the user selected fast speed in the observed right-social meal. IF dining context includes a social partner in front of the user AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a simple dipped savory meal such as chicken nuggets with ketchup, THEN fast BECAUSE the observed front-social meal shows the user favors efficient motion in that subtype rather than the slower speeds seen in some other social settings.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is bite-sized pizza, sandwich-like pieces, cut fruit pieces, or other segmented items where a narrower bite may help THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user selected parallel skewering for bite-sized pizza in personal dining and previously for sandwich pieces and cut fruit, suggesting a recurring preference for narrower bites on piecewise foods. IF dining context includes watching TV in front OR watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is bite-sized sandwiches THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user selected parallel skewering in those meals, suggesting a preference for narrower bites for sandwich pieces. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is cut fruit pieces such as cantaloupe, banana, or watermelon THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user selected parallel skewering in the observed left-TV fruit meal. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is simple fresh fruit pieces such as cantaloupe, banana, or watermelon THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the repeated left-social fruit observation supports a stable preference for narrower fruit bites in that context. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a dipped savory meal such as fish bites with tartar sauce THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected perpendicular skewering in the observed personal dipped lunch, consistent with a preference for acquisition reliability on dipped savory foods. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a simple savory meal, a dipped savory meal such as fish bites with tartar sauce, a sauced mixed dish, or fresh fruit pieces such as cantaloupe, banana, or watermelon THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected this option across repeated front-facing meals and appears to favor acquisition reliability when attention is forward. IF dining context includes working on a laptop in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a savory meal with a simple dip such as ketchup THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected this option in the observed front-laptop meal. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm savory meal with a dip such as nuggets with ketchup THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE observed left-TV ketchup evidence supports the pattern that dipped savory foods favor acquisition reliability. IF dining context includes working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a straightforward warm savory meal without sauces or dips, such as chicken breast strips and hash brown, THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the observed left-laptop meal extends the pattern that simple warm savory working meals favor reliable bite acquisition. IF dining context includes working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm savory meal with a separate dip or sauce, such as breaded fish bites with tartar sauce, THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the repeated left-laptop dipped meal reinforces the same reliability-first skewering preference in that context. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm sauced savory entree OR a simple warm dipped savory meal such as chicken nuggets with ketchup THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE left-social savory meals consistently favor acquisition reliability. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right OR working on a laptop on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is cold fruit, fruit dessert, or a warm savory entree such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected perpendicular skewering in observed right-attention meals, including repeated right-laptop savory meals. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes bananas, brownies, or chocolate sauce THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected perpendicular skewering in the observed right-social meal. IF dining context includes a social partner in front of the user AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a simple dipped savory meal such as chicken nuggets with ketchup, THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the observed front-social meal matches the broader reliability-first pattern for front-facing and dip-associated savory foods.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm personal meal, including dipped fish bites or bite-sized pizza and broccoli, THEN no BECAUSE the user skipped confirmation in observed personal lunches, indicating that in private settings the user prioritizes efficiency. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a simple savory meal without sauces or dips THEN no BECAUSE the user skipped confirmation in repeated front-TV straightforward savory meals, favoring lower interaction burden when bite acquisition is likely easy. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a sauced mixed dish, bite-sized sandwiches, a dipped savory meal such as fish bites with tartar sauce, or fresh fruit pieces such as cantaloupe, banana, or watermelon THEN yes BECAUSE the user requested explicit bite-acquisition confirmation in those front-TV meals, indicating extra verification is preferred when bite quality may be less predictable or when attention is directed elsewhere. IF dining context includes working on a laptop in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a savory meal with a dip or sauce such as ketchup THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected confirmation in the observed front-laptop dipped lunch. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert items with potentially messier or less predictable acquisition, bite-sized sandwiches, or warm savory food with ketchup THEN yes BECAUSE the user explicitly requested confirmation in observed left-TV meals. IF dining context includes working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a straightforward warm savory meal without sauces or dips, such as chicken breast strips and hash brown, THEN yes BECAUSE the observed left-laptop meal shows that while working on the left, the user still prefers explicit bite verification even for a simple lunch. IF dining context includes working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm savory meal with a separate dip or sauce, such as breaded fish bites with tartar sauce, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated left-laptop evidence reinforces a stable preference for explicit bite verification while working on the left. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm sauced savory entree THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected confirmation in the observed left-social sauced meal. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a simple warm dipped savory meal such as chicken nuggets with ketchup THEN no BECAUSE the new left-social meal shows that in this simpler familiar subtype the user prioritizes efficiency over explicit bite verification. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is simple fresh fruit pieces THEN no BECAUSE the user skipped confirmation in the observed left-TV fruit meal. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is simple fresh fruit pieces such as cantaloupe, banana, or watermelon THEN no BECAUSE repeated left-social fruit meals show that for easy cold fruit the user favors efficiency over explicit verification even in a social-left setting. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is simple cold fruit, fruit-and-cream items, or a straightforward warm savory entree without a separate dip THEN no BECAUSE the user skipped confirmation in observed right-TV meals. IF dining context includes working on a laptop on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm savory entree without a separate dip, including General Tso's chicken and broccoli, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated right-laptop observations for the same savory meal support a stable preference for explicit bite verification while working on the right. IF dining context includes working on a laptop on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is simple cold fruit or fruit dessert THEN no BECAUSE prior right-laptop evidence with simpler cold items favored efficiency over confirmation. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes bananas, brownies, or chocolate sauce THEN no BECAUSE the user skipped confirmation in the observed right-social meal. IF dining context includes a social partner in front of the user AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a simple dipped savory meal such as chicken nuggets with ketchup, THEN no BECAUSE the observed front-social meal shows that in this subtype the user prioritizes efficiency over explicit bite verification.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to lack of trunk control, so outside-mouth transfer is not physically compatible.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user's physical abilities support inside-mouth transfer rather than outside-mouth transfer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm personal meal, including dipped fish bites or bite-sized pizza and broccoli, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected a salient multimodal cue in observed personal lunches. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a simple savory meal without sauces or dips THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED-only signaling in repeated front-TV straightforward meals. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a sauced mixed dish, bite-sized sandwiches, a dipped savory meal such as fish bites with tartar sauce, or fresh fruit pieces such as cantaloupe, banana, or watermelon THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected a more salient multimodal cue in those front-TV meals, suggesting added prompting when the meal is more complex or attention is divided. IF dining context includes working on a laptop in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a savory meal with a dip or sauce such as ketchup THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected multimodal readiness signaling in the observed front-laptop dipped lunch. IF dining context includes watching TV on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert, cold fruit, bite-sized sandwich items, or warm savory food with ketchup THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected multimodal readiness cues in left-TV contexts. IF dining context includes working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a straightforward warm savory meal without sauces or dips, such as chicken breast strips and hash brown, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the observed left-laptop meal indicates the user prefers a salient multimodal cue while working on the left even for a simple meal. IF dining context includes working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm savory meal with a separate dip or sauce, such as breaded fish bites with tartar sauce, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE repeated left-laptop evidence reinforces the same multimodal readiness-cue preference. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm sauced savory entree THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED-only readiness signaling in the observed left-social meal. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a simple warm dipped savory meal such as chicken nuggets with ketchup THEN LED BECAUSE the new left-social meal matches the quieter cue style already seen in left-social savory dining. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is simple fresh fruit pieces such as cantaloupe, banana, or watermelon THEN speech + LED BECAUSE repeated left-social fruit evidence supports a multimodal cue preference for that subtype. IF dining context includes watching TV on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is simple cold fruit, fruit dessert, or a straightforward warm savory entree without a separate dip THEN LED BECAUSE earlier right-attention meals often used LED-only signaling. IF dining context includes working on a laptop on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a substantial warm savory entree such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN speech + LED BECAUSE repeated right-laptop savory observations support a stable preference for more salient multimodal readiness cues in that subtype. IF dining context includes working on a laptop on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is simple cold fruit or fruit dessert THEN LED BECAUSE prior right-laptop evidence with simpler cold items favored LED-only signaling. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes bananas, brownies, or chocolate sauce THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED-only readiness signaling in the observed right-social meal. IF dining context includes a social partner in front of the user AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a simple dipped savory meal such as chicken nuggets with ketchup, THEN LED BECAUSE the observed front-social meal aligns front-social interaction with a quieter LED-only cue style.

IF dining context includes a social partner in front of the user AND affective state is neutral or unspecified/unknown AND food has no special sauce/dip relevance OR is a simple dipped savory meal, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE repeated front-social observations show the user prefers low-effort automated initiation in that context. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food includes bananas, brownies, or chocolate sauce THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automated initiation in the observed right-social meal. convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a warm personal meal, including dipped fish bites or bite-sized pizza and broccoli, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected a salient multimodal completion cue in observed personal lunches. IF dining context includes watching TV in front AND affective state is unspecified/unknown AND food is a simple savory meal without sauces or dips THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED-only completion signaling in repeated front-TV straightforward meals.
